channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Apple wants permission to buy memory from a blacklisted Chinese supplier
item.description: Apple is looking to alleviate some of the pressure on its supply chain by seeking an exception from the Trump administration to buy RAM chips from CXMT, a company blacklisted by the Pentagon over...

item.title: Building an AI-Powered CRM System: A Practical Overview
item.description: Most CRM platforms do a good job of storing customer information, managing opportunities, and tracking interactions. But once teams start handling thousands of leads, support tickets, and...

item.title: How to Build a Compliant Fintech App in the UAE
item.description: The UAE fintech sector spans three distinct regulatory authorities. Whether you are building for the CBUAE-licensed mainland, the DIFC, or the ADGM, the engineering obligations for KYC, AML, data...

item.title: How we architect a real-time fleet tracking platform
item.description: Fleet tracking looks simple from the outside: dots moving on a map. The engineering underneath is not. This is a reference build that walks the full delivery cycle, and the non-functional details...

item.title: BRD vs SRS vs SDD: which document do you actually need?
item.description: Three documents define most software builds, and they are not interchangeable. Here is what a business requirements document, a software requirements specification, and a software design...

item.title: Largest Rectangle In Histogram
item.description: leetcode.com Problem Statement Given an array heights[] representing the height of histogram bars, find the area of the largest rectangle that can be formed. Each bar has: Width = 1 Brute Force...

item.title: Sequence Transduction: The Forgotten Problem That Led to Modern LLMs
item.description: Hello, I'm Shrijith Venkatramana. I'm building git-lrc, an AI code reviewer that runs on every commit. Star Us to help devs discover the project. Do give it a try and share your feedback for...

item.title: ☁️ What Even Is "The Cloud"?
item.description: Every now and then, I want to slow down, pick one cloud concept, and break it down properly — no jargon, just the kind of explanation you'd get from a friend who actually knows this stuff. Today's...

item.title: I Audited My AWS Account and Here's What I Found
item.description: I finally sat down and did a proper audit of my AWS account. I knew I had projects running and services connected to my domains, but I had never actually gone line by line through the bill. When I...

item.title: Supabase (YC S20) Is Hiring for Multigres
item.description: Article URL: https://jobs.ashbyhq.com/supabase/2e718684-4f75-4a99-8d6b-3b6bd44e4228 Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48699842 Points: 0 # Comments: 0

item.title: I Built a Free Browser-Based Urdu Writing and Design Platform
item.description: I Built a Free Browser-Based Urdu Writing and Design Platform iloveurdu.com I want to tell you about a tool I built for Urdu writers, students, designers, and content creators. It's called I Love...

item.title: Apple Vision Pro exec is reportedly leaving for OpenAI
item.description: Paul Meade, the Apple vice president in charge of the Vision Pro headset, is reportedly leaving the company to join OpenAI’s hardware team.

item.title: The Guardian’s Kai Wright refuses to buy a new phone
item.description: Kai Wright is the co-host of Stateside with Kai and Carter over at the Guardian. But Wright has been bringing his unique insights to listeners for years. He's also hosted Notes From America, The...

item.title: The fittest founder in the room got cancer. Here’s how he used AI to fight back.
item.description: When confronted with cancer, Connor Christou fed everything tied tied to his regime — blood results, scan data, wearable output, journal entries — into Claude.

item.title: Indie developers got tired of waiting for a new Star Fox, so they’re making their own
item.description: Nostalgia remains a powerful force. So much so that, in exploring the echoes of a late-'90s childhood spent skimming the water of Corneria and sneering "cocky little freaks!" in time with a monkey...

item.title: Why is Apple asking me to pay more for Big Tech’s AI obsession?
item.description: Tim Cook recently said price increases were "unavoidable" and described the company's pricing as "unsustainable." The 16-inch MacBook Pro saw its price go up by $300. The 11-inch iPad Air went...

item.title: Inside the room where the smart home industry is still betting on Matter
item.description: Four years ago, overlooking a canal in Amsterdam, the smart home industry collectively launched Matter, the one interoperability standard to rule them all. Heralded as the solution to the...

item.title: OpenAI Previews GPT-5.6 Sol With Restricted Access and Stronger Cyber Safeguards
item.description: OpenAI on Friday released three versions of GPT-5.6, called Sol, Terra, and Luna, as a limited preview to a small number of companies as part of an ongoing engagement with the U.S. government....

item.title: Asian AI startups launch Mythos-like models as Anthropic’s export ban drags on
item.description: New models are launching in Asia that promise Mythos-like capabilities without fear of an export ban. U.S. AI labs may never recover this enormous market.

item.title: This puzzle game’s simple premise hides surprising depth
item.description: What's the Password? has a simple concept: To solve each of the game's more than 100 puzzles, you have to type in the right four-digit password on a number pad. That might sound like a limited...

item.title: This might be the new best smart speaker
item.description: Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 134, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, hope you're okay in all this heat, and also you can read all the...

item.title: Prime Day is almost over, but these are still the best Apple deals I’ve seen
item.description: Amazon’s Prime Day is now in its final hours, but whether you’re looking for a new pair of wireless earbuds or a smartwatch, there’s a good chance you’ll still find a discount. The Apple Watch...

item.title: It’s the last day of Prime Day — here are over 140 great deals to choose from
item.description: We’ve arrived at the final day of Prime Day, which at this point should probably be called “Prime Week.” We’ve found discounts on all manner of gadgets, including TVs, smart home tech, chargers,...

item.title: 24 Prime Day deals Verge readers are grabbing before Prime Day ends
item.description: There are an overwhelming number of Prime Day deals to sort through, which is why we spend so much time highlighting products we’ve already tested and can stand behind. But our recommendations are...

item.title: Trump Admin releases Anthropic Mythos to be used by more than 100 US companies, agencies
item.description: Over 100 companies and government agencies are reportedly authorized to use Mythos 5, including their non-American employees.

item.title: Trump Administration Allows Anthropic to Release Mythos to Select US Organizations
item.description: After weeks of negotiations, the White House permitted Anthropic to grant access to its most advanced AI model to a select group of US companies and government agencies.

item.title: The Chinese Control the Majority of Argentina’s Squid Fleet
item.description: Chinese companies control nearly two-thirds of Argentina’s own squid fleet.

item.title: FBI Warns Russian Intelligence Hackers Target Signal Backup Recovery Keys
item.description: The FBI and CISA have updated their March warning about Russian intelligence phishing Signal accounts, and the operators have added a step: they now coax targets into handing over their Signal...

item.title: OpenAI limits GPT-5.6 rollout after government request, says restrictions shouldn’t be the norm
item.description: “We don’t believe this kind of government access process should become the long-term default,” says OpenAI. “It keeps the best tools from users, developers, enterprises, cyber defenders, and...

item.title: OpenAI poaches Uber India chief to lead its biggest market outside the US
item.description: The hire marks OpenAI's latest push into India, expanding offices, partnerships and hiring.
